title: Mirabilis nyctaginea
intro: Mirabilis nyctaginea, also known as the Four O'Clock, is a flowering plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its vibrant, trumpet-shaped flowers that bloom in the late afternoon and evening. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis nyctaginea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the Four O'Clock is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Mirabilis nyctaginea is a species of flowering plant in the four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae). It is commonly known as the moonflower or four o'clock flower. This plant is native to North America and is often found growing in dry to moist areas. Mirabilis nyctaginea is classified as an annual or perennial herb, depending on the climate and region. It belongs to the genus Mirabilis within the family Nyctaginaceae.
uses: Mirabilis nyctaginea, also known as the four o'clock flower or marvel of Peru, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, the roots of the plant are used to treat fever and other illnesses. Furthermore, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antiviral properties. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies, as their effectiveness and safety can vary depending on the individual and the specific condition being treated.

constituents: Mirabilis nyctaginea contains various active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
